Convolvulus arvensis, also known as bindweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce pain, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Convolvulus arvensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Convolvulus arvensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.
